description: Intensifier 'ru' raises the degree of the descriptor or verb it precedes. The forms 'ru welao' and 'ru reshi' mean very good and very fast. Comparison belongs to 'mo' (CMPR).
articulatory_notes: A tongue-tip tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge leads into high-back 'u'. The lips round for the vowel, and the complete particle carries full stress.
sound_symbolism: The tap or trill adds a flick of motion before the deep rounded vowel. Its contour may sound like a compact swell.
usage_notes: The particle sits in the degree position nearest the word it modifies. It can precede comparative 'mo', so 'ru mo welao' means much better.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The particle puts degree into the grammar. A strongly felt claim remains free to use conversational volume.
